query: Sanae Takaichi immigration rules January 2026 Japan new regulations specifics

mako.raw:
{"query":"Sanae Takaichi immigration rules January 2026 Japan new regulations specifics","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1792,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.cfr.org/articles/political-realignment-and-the-2026-japanese-election","title":"Japan's 2026 Election: Immigration Reform | Council on Foreign Relations","content":"She hopes to take advantage of her continued high approval ratings which hovered around 70 percent for months to better position the Liberal Democratic Party (LDP) to legislate a slate of policy initiatives in the coming year.
With Japan’s ongoing labor shortages, demand for foreign workers resulted in a record 2.3 million in 2024, and that number is expected to be even higher for 2025.
These arriving workers come mostly from Asian countries, including Vietnam (24.8 percent), China (17.8 percent), and the Philippines (10.7 percent).
Takaichi has been an advocate for Japan’s traditional values and social practices, and this priority informs her broader framing of Japan’s future.
Without a doubt, Sanseitō’s rise pushed the issue of foreigners into the national spotlight.
A November 2025 Asahi Shimbun poll found that 56 percent of their respondents believe Japan needs fewer visitors and immigrants and 66 percent back Prime Minister Takaichi’s tougher stance.
In the FY 2026 Tax Reform Framework, the LDP and Ishin proposed tripling the international departure tax from ¥1,000 to ¥3,000."},{"url":"https://thediplomat.com/2025/11/japans-takaichi-pushes-stricter-rules-on-foreign-nationals/","title":"Japan’s Takaichi Pushes Stricter Rules on Foreign Nationals – The Diplomat","content":"Prime Minister Takaichi Sanae’s government held its first ministerial meeting on policies affecting foreign nationals on November 4 and plans to compile basic views on policies by January 2026.
The revisions will likely cover issues such as unpaid insurance premiums, visa overstays, land purchases, and illegal logging.
Her push for stricter enforcement was in line with a campaign pledge during the Liberal Democratic Party presidential race.
Another issue is traffic accidents involving foreign drivers, which had already prompted new rules in October."},{"url":"https://eastasiaforum.org/2026/05/15/japan-narrows-path-to-citizenship-amid-labour-shortages/","title":"Japan narrows path to citizenship amid labour shortages | East Asia Forum","content":"But under Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi, naturalisation appears to be becoming harder in practice, with applicants facing closer scrutiny of their employment history, tax payments and health insurance compliance.
Under Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi, the Nationality Act has not been formally amended — Article 5 still states that applicants for naturalisation must have ‘continuously maintained a domicile in Japan for five years or more’.
By the end of 2025, SSW visa holders reached record numbers, with strong growth in sectors facing chronic labour shortages.
Under Takaichi, this balancing act has become more ideologically explicit.
The SSW system was designed as a labour mobility framework rather than an immigration pathway."},{"url":"https://www.nippon.com/en/in-depth/d01206/","title":"Japan’s “Foreigner Policy” Skirts Key Issues: No Orderly Coexistence Without Plan for Immigration | Nippon.com","content":"In January 2026, the government of Prime Minister Takaichi Sanae unveiled a policy framework titled Comprehensive Measures for Accepting Foreign Nationals and Orderly Coexistence.
At the same time, the Takaichi administration has had very little to say about the real policy issues—that is, the government’s basic approach to the use of foreign workers to meet Japanese industry’s labor needs amid an aging population.
Takaichi administration’s comprehensive measures aim to tighten the requirements for obtaining legal recognition as a permanent resident of Japan or naturalized Japanese citizen."}]}

{"query":"Sanae Takaichi immigration rules January 2026 Japan new regulations specifics","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://www.nippon.com/en/in-depth/d01206","title":"Japan’s “Foreigner Policy” Skirts Key Issues: No Orderly Coexistence Without Plan for Immigration | Nippon.com","content":"* Japan’s “Foreigner Policy” Skirts Key Issues: No Orderly Coexistence Without Plan for Immigration. The author argues that the Takaichi administration’s new policy framework takes a short-sighted scattergun approach, addressing unrelated controversies while ignoring the long-term consequences of an immigration policy that rejects the concept of immigration. In January 2026, the government of Prime Minister Takaichi Sanae unveiled a policy framework titled Comprehensive Measures for Accepting Foreign Nationals and Orderly Coexistence. With regard to foreign nationals currently residing in Japan, the government has termed its approach a policy of “orderly coexistence.” On the one hand, this involves a continuation of integration policies adopted in 2018, such as Japanese language training for foreign workers and educational and vocational assistance for their children. At the same time, the Takaichi administration has had very little to say about the real policy issues—that is, the government’s basic approach to the use of foreign workers to meet Japanese industry’s labor needs amid an aging population.","score":0.7570233,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://mainichi.jp/english/articles/20251221/p2a/00m/0na/001000c","title":"Gov't plans stricter rules for foreigners living in Japan","content":"The new plan will be ready in January 2026. The government plans to change the rule from "five years or more" living in Japan to "10 years or","score":0.6664756,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=FNjkU6II8Us","title":"Japan’s 2026 Immigration Policy: Progress Update on Visas, PR, Naturalization & JESTA","content":"Japan’s 2026 Immigration Policy: Progress Update on Visas, PR, Naturalization & JESTA

📌 Japan's 2026 Immigration Policy — Progress Update
Following up on the "Comprehensive Measures" decided January 23, here's where each policy stands as of end of March 2026.
🏢 Business Manager visa: new applications down ~96% (capital requirement ¥5M → ¥30M)
🛂 "JESTA" pre-travel authorization bill submitted, targeting 2028 rollout
🏠 Permanent Residency: 3-year fast-track ends, 5-year rule takes effect April 2027
❌ PR revocation for unpaid taxes/pension/insurance from April 2027
🇯🇵 Naturalization residency requirement: 5 years → 10 years from April 2026
💰 Residence fees rising — renewals up to ¥100,000, Permanent Residency up to ¥300,000

But under Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi, naturalisation appears to be becoming harder in practice, with applicants facing closer scrutiny of their employment history, tax payments and health insurance compliance. As Japan’s foreign workforce reaches record numbers, citizenship and permanent membership in the national community are becoming more restrictive — separating market participation from belonging. Under Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi, the *Nationality Act* has not been formally amended — Article 5 still states that applicants for naturalisation must have ‘continuously maintained a domicile in Japan for five years or more’. But in practice, applicants increasingly face expectations of stable residence and social integration in Japan for closer to a decade before naturalisation is realistically attainable.
